text.title: E2E Framework Migration Playbook Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every E2E Framework Migration Playbook application must enforce. These are HARD rules — implementation expedience does not override them.

rule.statement: Wave 3 cutover requires every targeted module to have a passing parity test pair (old framework + new framework producing equivalent observable behaviour).
rule.rationale: Cutover without parity means regressions hide until customers find them; parity tests are the only objective gate.

rule.statement: Wave 2 prefers automated codemods (jscodeshift, libcst, ast-grep) over hand-edits for any transformation affecting >=10 files.
rule.rationale: Hand-edits at scale introduce drift; codemods are reviewable transformations applied uniformly.

rule.statement: During Wave 2, both old and new code paths are exercised in CI by parity tests until decommission is approved.
rule.rationale: Single-path testing during dual-run misses regressions in whichever side stops running; running both keeps both honest.

rule.statement: Every wave has a documented rollback procedure with maximum 30 minutes time-to-rollback. No wave is started without it.
rule.rationale: Migration risk is dominated by the absence of rollback; documenting the path forces the failure modes into the open.

rule.statement: Wave 4 decommission is part of the playbook, not optional. Leaving old framework in 'just in case' is not a wave exit.
rule.rationale: Skipped decommission compounds debt and prevents future migrations; the fourth wave closes the migration.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies-If hold and no Skip-If trigger applies, walk the procedure end-to-end and run the validator before declaring done.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory run leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The run leaf maps "preconditions met" to the canonical execution path.

rule.statement: Skip this methodology when the Applies-If preconditions are not met OR any Skip-If disqualifier holds.

rationale.source: F-066 v3 B5.6 — mandatory skip leaf

rule.rationale: Decision-tree leaves must reference a rule id. The skip leaf needs a real rule so non-applicable inputs route cleanly out instead of producing false-positive verdicts.
